Detect runway incursions or report failu.goal: Detect runway incursions or report failure
Detect runway incursions or report failu.strategy: The goal can be achieved by ensuring the detection system is reliable and the reporting system is functional.

subgoals.G1.1: The detection system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The reporting system is functional.

RSM data inadequacy detection is impleme.goal: RSM data inadequacy detection is implemented with required dependability.
RSM data inadequacy detection is impleme.strategy: The dependability of RSM data inadequacy detection can be determined by examining its components and their reliability.

subgoals.G1.1: The data collection system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The data analysis algorithm is accurate.
subgoals.G1.3: The alert system functions correctly.

Ownship runway locator reports failure w.goal: Ownship runway locator reports failure with p>=p2 if quality of information is inaccurate.
Ownship runway locator reports failure w.strategy: The accuracy of the ownship runway locator can be determined by examining the quality of information it receives.

subgoals.G1.1: The ownship runway locator receives accurate information.
subgoals.G1.2: The ownship runway locator reports failure with a probability greater than or equal to p2.

solutions.Sn1.1: Data validation techniques, comparison with ground truth.
solutions.Sn1.2: Failure analysis, statistical analysis of failure probabilities.

Conflict detector reports failure with p.goal: Conflict detector reports failure with p>=p3 if quality of information is inadequate.
Conflict detector reports failure with p.strategy: The adequacy of information quality can be determined by examining the reliability and accuracy of the data sources.

subgoals.G1.1: The data sources are reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The data sources are accurate.

solutions.Sn1.1: Data source reliability assessments, historical data analysis.
solutions.Sn1.2: Data source accuracy evaluations, data validation techniques.

Traffic positions component reports fail.goal: Traffic positions component reports failure with p>=9 if quality of information is inadequate.
Traffic positions component reports fail.strategy: The failure of the traffic positions component can be determined by examining the quality of information it receives.

subgoals.G1.1: The quality of information received by the traffic positions component is adequate.

solutions.Sn1.1: Data validation reports, data integrity checks.

Traffic positions component correctly im.goal: Traffic positions component correctly implements reasonableness checks and error detection protocol.
Traffic positions component correctly im.strategy: The correctness of the traffic positions component can be determined by examining its reasonableness checks and error detection protocol.

subgoals.G1.1: The reasonableness checks are implemented correctly.
subgoals.G1.2: The error detection protocol is implemented correctly.

solutions.Sn1.1: Code reviews, unit tests for reasonableness checks.
solutions.Sn1.2: Code reviews, unit tests for error detection protocol.

UAVs flying in the managed airspace will.goal: UAVs flying in the managed airspace will not collide with fixed ground-based obstacles (FGB)
UAVs flying in the managed airspace will.strategy: The goal can be achieved by ensuring the UAVs have obstacle detection and avoidance capabilities.

subgoals.G1.1: The UAVs have reliable obstacle detection sensors.
subgoals.G1.2: The UAVs have effective obstacle avoidance algorithms.

solutions.Sn1.1: Manufacturer test reports, third-party evaluations of obstacle detection sensors.
solutions.Sn1.2: Code audits, simulation results of obstacle avoidance algorithms.

Comply to infrastructure safety case (IS.goal: Comply to infrastructure safety case (ISAC) requirements.
Comply to infrastructure safety case (IS.strategy: Compliance to ISAC requirements can be achieved by addressing specific sub-requirements.

subgoals.G1.1: Addressing the requirements related to system reliability.
subgoals.G1.2: Addressing the requirements related to system security.
subgoals.G1.3: Addressing the requirements related to system maintainability.

solutions.Sn1.3: Maintenance plans, documentation on system updates.

UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based object.goal: UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based objects.
UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based object.strategy: The avoidance of fixed Ground-Based objects can be achieved by examining the UAV's sensing capabilities and navigation algorithms.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor system can detect fixed Ground-Based objects.
subgoals.G1.2: The navigation algorithm can calculate avoidance trajectories.

Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) prov.goal: Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) provides correct instructions for avoiding collisions between two UAVs.
Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) prov.strategy: The correctness of the Collision Avoidance Algorithm can be determined by examining its inputs, outputs, and internal logic.

subgoals.G1.1: The inputs to the CAA are accurate and reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The outputs of the CAA are correct and safe.
subgoals.G1.3: The internal logic of the CAA is sound and free from errors.

GPS is accurate within 5 cms..strategy: The accuracy of GPS can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

subgoals.G1.1: The GPS receiver is precise.
subgoals.G1.2: The GPS algorithm is accurate.

The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery.goal: The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery is sufficiently mitigated.
The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery.strategy: The hazard of running out of battery can be mitigated by ensuring the availability of charging infrastructure and implementing efficient power management strategies.

subgoals.G1.1: There is a sufficient number of charging stations available.
subgoals.G1.2: The self-driving car has an efficient power management system.

solutions.Sn1.1: Analysis of charging station density in the target area, availability of charging station maps.
solutions.Sn1.2: Documentation of power management algorithms, simulation results showing energy efficiency.

All identified causes for the hazard, El.goal: All identified causes for the hazard, El1, are sufficiently managed.
All identified causes for the hazard, El.strategy: The hazard El1 can be sufficiently managed by addressing its identified causes.

subgoals.G1.1: Identify all causes for the hazard El1.
subgoals.G1.2: Develop strategies to manage each identified cause.

The applied mitigations lead to sufficie.goal: The applied mitigations lead to sufficient risk reduction of the hazard El1.
The applied mitigations lead to sufficie.strategy: The risk reduction of hazard El1 can be determined by examining the effectiveness of the applied mitigations.

subgoals.G1.1: The mitigations effectively reduce the likelihood of hazard El1.
subgoals.G1.2: The mitigations effectively reduce the severity of hazard El1.

solutions.Sn1.1: Test results, historical data showing reduced likelihood of hazard El1.
solutions.Sn1.2: Test results, historical data showing reduced severity of hazard El1.

The mitigation requirement R1 holds.strategy: The effectiveness of the mitigation measure can be demonstrated through evidence.

subgoals.G1.1: The mitigation measure is implemented correctly.
subgoals.G1.2: The mitigation measure reduces the risk to an acceptable level.

Contract-based formal verification shows.goal: Contract-based formal verification shows with sufficient confidence that R1 is achieved in the design of Inspection Rover.
Contract-based formal verification shows.strategy: Contract-based formal verification can provide evidence that R1 is achieved in the design of Inspection Rover.

subgoals.G1.1: The contract-based formal verification is performed correctly.
subgoals.G1.2: The contract specifies that R1 is achieved in the design of Inspection Rover.

solutions.Sn1.1: Verification reports, documentation of the verification process.
solutions.Sn1.2: Contract documentation, formal specification of R1.

System remains functional for intended r.goal: System remains functional for intended radiation environment(NASA R&M mod) in order to complete science mission objective: Record the number of upsets in 28nm bulk SRAM in LEO for a period of 1 year.
System remains functional for intended r.strategy: The system's functionality in the intended radiation environment can be determined by examining its hardware components and their ability to withstand radiation.

subgoals.G1.1: The hardware components are radiation-hardened.
subgoals.G1.2: The system can record the number of upsets in 28nm bulk SRAM.
subgoals.G1.3: The system can operate in LEO for a period of 1 year.

solutions.Sn1.1: Manufacturer radiation-hardening certifications, radiation test reports.
solutions.Sn1.2: System specifications, documentation showing recording capability.
solutions.Sn1.3: System specifications, documentation showing LEO operation capability.

System and its elements are designed to.goal: System and its elements are designed to withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses (radiation) for the life of the mission (NASA R&M).
System and its elements are designed to.strategy: The system's ability to withstand loads and stresses can be determined by examining its design and materials.

subgoals.G1.1: The system's design is robust.
subgoals.G1.2: The materials used in the system are durable.
subgoals.G1.3: The system can withstand radiation exposure.

System is tolerant to radiation-induced.goal: System is tolerant to radiation-induced faults and failures (NASA R&M mod).
System is tolerant to radiation-induced.strategy: The system's tolerance to radiation-induced faults and failures can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

subgoals.G1.1: The hardware components are radiation-hardened.
subgoals.G1.2: The software components are designed to handle radiation-induced faults.
subgoals.G1.3: The system has built-in fault detection and recovery mechanisms.

solutions.Sn1.1: Radiation testing reports for hardware components.
solutions.Sn1.2: Code reviews, simulation results for software components.
solutions.Sn1.3: Documentation showing fault detection and recovery mechanisms.

System is able to recover from anomalies.goal: System is able to recover from anomalies affecting functions that are important to top-level expectations (NASA R&M).
System is able to recover from anomalies.strategy: The system's ability to recover from anomalies can be determined by examining its fault tolerance and error handling mechanisms.

subgoals.G1.1: The system has fault tolerance mechanisms in place.
subgoals.G1.2: The system has effective error handling mechanisms.

solutions.Sn1.1: Redundancy in hardware components, fault injection testing.
solutions.Sn1.2: Error logging and reporting, exception handling mechanisms.

COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requi.goal: COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requirement: No latch-up seen up to 5e9 protons/cm2 for 200 MeV protons.
COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requi.strategy: The COTS parts have been tested and proven to withstand the specified proton radiation levels without experiencing latch-up.

subgoals.G1.1: The COTS parts can withstand proton radiation up to 5e9 protons/cm2 for 200 MeV protons without latch-up.

solutions.Sn1.1: Radiation testing reports, latch-up test results for COTS parts under specified proton radiation levels.

We see a world where everyone who choose.goal: We see a world where everyone who chooses to use these services, as well as those who do not, have confidence in a safe and secure sector.
We see a world where everyone who choose.strategy: To achieve confidence in a safe and secure sector, we need to address various aspects of safety and security.

subgoals.G1.1: Ensure data privacy and protection.
subgoals.G1.2: Prevent unauthorized access and cyber attacks.
subgoals.G1.3: Maintain system reliability and availability.
subgoals.G1.4: Establish trust and transparency in the sector.

solutions.Sn1.1: Implement strong encryption and data protection measures.
solutions.Sn1.2: Implement robust access control mechanisms and security protocols.
solutions.Sn1.3: Implement redundancy and failover mechanisms for high availability.
solutions.Sn1.4: Establish regulatory frameworks, conduct audits, and provide clear information about security measures.

There is justified confidence that cyber.goal: There is justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services.
There is justified confidence that cyber.strategy: The confidence in cyber-security can be determined by examining the security measures in place and the resilience of the regulated services.

subgoals.G1.1: The security measures are effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The regulated services are resilient.

solutions.Sn1.2: Incident response plans, disaster recovery plans.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services.
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: The confidence in cyber-security can be determined by examining the security measures in place for UK regulated services.

subgoals.G1.1: The security measures are effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The risks posed by cyber-security issues are acceptable.

Other stakeholders have justified confid.goal: Other stakeholders have justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services.
Other stakeholders have justified confid.strategy: The confidence in cyber-security can be determined by examining the security measures and incident response capabilities of the regulated services.

subgoals.G1.1: The security measures are effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The incident response capabilities are robust.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services at present.
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: The confidence in cyber-security can be determined by examining the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services.

subgoals.G1.1: The safety of the UK regulated services is ensured.
subgoals.G1.2: The resilience of the UK regulated services is ensured.

solutions.Sn1.1: Safety assessment reports, incident response plans.
solutions.Sn1.2: Resilience testing results, disaster recovery plans.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues will not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services in the future.
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: The confidence in cyber-security can be determined by examining the measures in place to mitigate risks and ensure safety and resilience.

subgoals.G1.1: The cyber-security measures are effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The safety and resilience of UK regulated services are maintained.

solutions.Sn1.2: Incident response plans, disaster recovery plans.

ARO regulatory activity provides justifi.goal: ARO regulatory activity provides justified confidence in the security and resilience.
ARO regulatory activity provides justifi.strategy: The ARO regulatory activity ensures that the system meets security and resilience standards.

subgoals.G1.1: The system meets the required security standards.
subgoals.G1.2: The system meets the required resilience standards.

ARO has justified confidence in the over.goal: ARO has justified confidence in the overall regulatory institutions and standards.
ARO has justified confidence in the over.strategy: The confidence in regulatory institutions and standards can be determined by examining their effectiveness and reliability.

subgoals.G1.1: The regulatory institutions are effective.
subgoals.G1.2: The regulatory standards are reliable.

ARO has justified confidence that the re.goal: ARO has justified confidence that the regulated system can deal with cyber related events and changes.
ARO has justified confidence that the re.strategy: The regulated system's ability to deal with cyber related events and changes can be determined by examining its security measures and response capabilities.

subgoals.G1.1: The system has robust security measures in place.
subgoals.G1.2: The system has effective response capabilities.

ARO has justified confidence in the tech.goal: ARO has justified confidence in the technical approaches for addressing cyber-security informed safety and resilience.
ARO has justified confidence in the tech.strategy: The confidence in the technical approaches can be determined by examining their effectiveness in addressing cyber-security, safety, and resilience.

subgoals.G1.1: The technical approaches effectively address cyber-security.
subgoals.G1.2: The technical approaches effectively address safety.
subgoals.G1.3: The technical approaches effectively address resilience.

solutions.Sn1.2: Safety analysis reports, incident response records.
solutions.Sn1.3: Resilience testing results, disaster recovery plans.

ARO provides other stakeholders with jus.goal: ARO provides other stakeholders with justified confidence in the regulated security and resilience.
ARO provides other stakeholders with jus.strategy: The confidence in ARO's security and resilience can be determined by examining its security measures and resilience capabilities.

subgoals.G1.1: ARO has robust security measures in place.
subgoals.G1.2: ARO has effective resilience capabilities.

solutions.Sn1.2: Disaster recovery plans, incident response plans.

Provides confidence existing system secu.goal: Provides confidence existing system secure and resilient
Provides confidence existing system secu.strategy: The security and resilience of the existing system can be determined by examining its security measures and response to incidents.

subgoals.G1.1: The system has robust access controls.
subgoals.G1.2: The system has effective intrusion detection and prevention mechanisms.
subgoals.G1.3: The system has a well-defined incident response plan.

solutions.Sn1.1: Security audit reports, access control policies.
solutions.Sn1.2: Intrusion detection system logs, firewall logs.
solutions.Sn1.3: Incident response plan documentation, incident reports.

Sources of doubt actively managed - actu.goal: Sources of doubt actively managed - actual and perceived risks
Sources of doubt actively managed - actu.strategy: The sources of doubt can be categorized into actual risks and perceived risks, and each category can be actively managed.

subgoals.G1.1: Actual risks are identified and mitigated.
subgoals.G1.2: Perceived risks are addressed and communicated effectively.

solutions.Sn1.1: Risk assessment reports, risk mitigation plans.

The vehicle is acceptably safe.strategy: The safety of the vehicle can be determined by examining its structural integrity, braking system, and collision avoidance system.

subgoals.G1.1: The vehicle's structural integrity is sufficient.
subgoals.G1.2: The braking system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.3: The collision avoidance system functions correctly.

solutions.Sn1.1: Structural integrity tests, crash test results.
solutions.Sn1.3: Collision avoidance system evaluations, real-world performance data.

Argument over the implementation of safe.goal: Argument over the implementation of safety requirements during product development
Argument over the implementation of safe.strategy: The argument can be broken down into sub-arguments related to different aspects of safety requirements implementation.

subgoals.G1.1: The safety requirements are clearly defined.
subgoals.G1.2: The safety requirements are properly communicated to the development team.
subgoals.G1.3: The safety requirements are effectively implemented in the product.

Argument over the implementation of safe.goal: Argument over the implementation of safety requirements post product development
Argument over the implementation of safe.strategy: The argument can be broken down into sub-arguments related to different aspects of safety requirements implementation.

subgoals.G1.1: The safety requirements are clearly defined.
subgoals.G1.2: The safety requirements are implemented correctly.
subgoals.G1.3: The safety requirements are verified and validated.

The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Sa.goal: The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Safety Requirements
The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Sa.strategy: The safety of the vehicle can be determined by examining its various safety features and systems.

subgoals.G1.1: The braking system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The collision avoidance system is effective.
subgoals.G1.3: The stability control system functions correctly.

System is acceptably safe to operate in.goal: System is acceptably safe to operate in the specified environment
System is acceptably safe to operate in.strategy: The safety of the system can be determined by examining its components and their performance in the specified environment.

subgoals.G1.1: The hardware components are reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The software components are robust.
subgoals.G1.3: The system operates within specified environmental conditions.

The residual risk associated with the id.goal: The residual risk associated with the identified system hazards has been reduced to an acceptable level.
The residual risk associated with the id.strategy: The residual risk can be reduced by addressing each identified system hazard.

subgoals.G1.1: Each identified system hazard has been mitigated.
subgoals.G1.2: The level of residual risk is acceptable.

solutions.Sn1.1: Implementation of safety measures to mitigate each identified hazard.
solutions.Sn1.2: Risk assessment reports, safety standards compliance.

Functional Safety: The residual risk of.goal: Functional Safety: The residual risk of (hazard T) is acceptable.
Functional Safety: The residual risk of.strategy: The residual risk of hazard T can be determined by examining its causes and the effectiveness of safety measures.

subgoals.G1.1: Identify the causes of hazard T.
subgoals.G1.2: Evaluate the effectiveness of safety measures in mitigating hazard T.

solutions.Sn1.2: Safety assessment, failure mode and effects analysis.

Mitigation: Implementation of Safety Goa.goal: Mitigation: Implementation of Safety Goal U has reduced the occurrence or propagation of hazard T causes
Mitigation: Implementation of Safety Goa.strategy: The reduction of hazard occurrence or propagation can be determined by examining the implementation of Safety Goal U.

subgoals.G1.1: Safety Goal U has been implemented correctly.

solutions.Sn1.1: Safety Goal U implementation documentation, verification and validation reports.

Detection and Diagnostics: Argument by a.goal: Detection and Diagnostics: Argument by appeal to detection and diagnostics of systematic and random failures within the design.
Detection and Diagnostics: Argument by a.strategy: The detection and diagnostics of failures can be achieved through analyzing the system's design and performance.

subgoals.G1.1: The system design includes built-in fault detection mechanisms.
subgoals.G1.2: The system can accurately diagnose and isolate failures.
subgoals.G1.3: The system can detect and diagnose both systematic and random failures.

solutions.Sn1.3: Failure mode and effects analysis, statistical analysis of failure data.

System Degradation: Argument by appeal o.goal: System Degradation: Argument by appeal over the transition to a safe system state in an appropriate time
System Degradation: Argument by appeal o.strategy: The system degradation can be determined by examining the time it takes to transition to a safe state.

subgoals.G1.1: The system can detect degradation.
subgoals.G1.2: The system can initiate a transition to a safe state.
subgoals.G1.3: The system can complete the transition within an appropriate time.

Random Failures: The impact of random fa.goal: Random Failures: The impact of random failures which can invalidate the Safety Goal U have been adequately managed.
Random Failures: The impact of random fa.strategy: The impact of random failures can be managed by implementing redundancy and fault tolerance measures.

subgoals.G1.1: Redundancy measures are in place.
subgoals.G1.2: Fault tolerance measures are in place.

solutions.Sn1.2: Error detection and correction mechanisms, failover systems.

A justified system safe state has been d.goal: A justified system safe state has been developed.
A justified system safe state has been d.strategy: The development of a justified system safe state can be achieved by ensuring the system meets certain safety requirements.

subgoals.G1.1: The system has undergone thorough hazard analysis.
subgoals.G1.2: The system has implemented safety mechanisms.
subgoals.G1.3: The system has been validated through testing and verification.

solutions.Sn1.1: Hazard analysis reports, risk assessment documentation.
solutions.Sn1.2: Safety mechanism design documents, implementation records.
solutions.Sn1.3: Test plans, test results, verification reports.

Restriction: Vehicle functionality has b.goal: Restriction: Vehicle functionality has been restricted.
Restriction: Vehicle functionality has b.strategy: The restriction of vehicle functionality can be determined by examining the specific features and capabilities that have been limited or disabled.

subgoals.G1.1: Certain driving modes or settings are disabled.
subgoals.G1.2: Specific vehicle functions are restricted or limited.

The implemented pacemaker software is ac.goal: The implemented pacemaker software is acceptably safe to operate in VVI mode.
The implemented pacemaker software is ac.strategy: The safety of the pacemaker software can be determined by examining its functionality and performance in VVI mode.

subgoals.G1.1: The software accurately detects and responds to heart signals.
subgoals.G1.2: The software operates within acceptable parameters.
subgoals.G1.3: The software does not introduce any additional risks.

solutions.Sn1.3: Risk analysis, failure mode and effects analysis (FMEA).

The implementation satisfies all the des.goal: The implementation satisfies all the desired safety properties within acceptable timing tolerances.
The implementation satisfies all the des.strategy: The satisfaction of safety properties can be determined by examining the implementation's adherence to safety requirements and timing constraints.

subgoals.G1.1: The implementation adheres to all safety requirements.
subgoals.G1.2: The implementation meets all timing constraints.

solutions.Sn1.1: Safety requirement verification reports, safety analysis results.
solutions.Sn1.2: Timing analysis reports, performance testing results.

The model satisfies all the desired prop.goal: The model satisfies all the desired properties.
The model satisfies all the desired prop.strategy: The model can be evaluated based on its individual properties.

subgoals.G1.1: The model is accurate.
subgoals.G1.2: The model is scalable.
subgoals.G1.3: The model is efficient.

solutions.Sn1.1: Comparison with ground truth data, statistical analysis.
solutions.Sn1.2: Performance testing with increasing data size.

The code generation process transforms t.goal: The code generation process transforms the model into the code correctly.
The code generation process transforms t.strategy: The correctness of the code generation process can be determined by examining the input model and the generated code.

subgoals.G1.1: The input model accurately represents the desired functionality.
subgoals.G1.2: The generated code accurately reflects the input model.

solutions.Sn1.2: Code reviews, code compilation and execution tests.

The synthesized code satisfies all the d.goal: The synthesized code satisfies all the desired properties with the timing tolerance.
The synthesized code satisfies all the d.strategy: The satisfaction of desired properties and timing tolerance can be determined by examining the code and its execution.

subgoals.G1.1: The code satisfies all the desired properties.
subgoals.G1.2: The code meets the timing tolerance.

solutions.Sn1.1: Code review reports, verification and validation results.
solutions.Sn1.2: Timing analysis reports, performance testing results.

Argument by use of a reliable tool set.goal: Argument by use of a reliable tool set.
Argument by use of a reliable tool set.strategy: The reliability of the tool set can be determined by examining its performance, stability, and user feedback.

subgoals.G1.1: The tool set performs its intended functions accurately.
subgoals.G1.2: The tool set is stable and does not crash frequently.
subgoals.G1.3: The tool set has positive user feedback.

Argument by harmless manual modification.goal: The self-driving car is safe to operate on public roads.
Argument by harmless manual modification.strategy: The safety of the self-driving car can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The navigation algorithm is accurate.
subgoals.G1.3: The emergency systems function correctly.

The code satisfies the property PropLRI.goal: The code satisfies the property PropLRI with timing tolerance.
The code satisfies the property PropLRI.strategy: The satisfaction of PropLRI can be determined by examining the code and its timing behavior.

subgoals.G1.1: The code correctly implements PropLRI.
subgoals.G1.2: The code meets the timing tolerance requirements.

The model satisfies the property PropHRI.goal: The model satisfies the property PropHRI with timing tolerance.
The model satisfies the property PropHRI.strategy: The satisfaction of PropHRI with timing tolerance can be determined by examining the model's timing behavior and its adherence to the specified property.

subgoals.G1.1: The model's timing behavior is within the specified tolerance.
subgoals.G1.2: The model adheres to the property PropHRI.

The code satisfies the property PropVRP.goal: The code satisfies the property PropVRP.
The code satisfies the property PropVRP.strategy: The code has been formally verified to satisfy the property PropVRP.

subgoals.G1.1: The code has undergone formal verification.
subgoals.G1.2: The property PropVRP has been specified correctly.

solutions.Sn1.1: Formal verification reports, proof of correctness.
solutions.Sn1.2: Formal specification of the property PropVRP.
